home.title: Published equipment guides built mobile-first.
home.description: Equip Blog organizes published medical equipment explainers into discovery pages for categories, manufacturers, equipment families, and individual posts.
home.featuredDescription: The lead article stays prominent on small screens first, then expands cleanly for tablet and desktop scanning.
home.latestDescription: Browse the newest approved guides without leaving the locale-prefixed public route structure.
home.discoveryDescription: Category, manufacturer, and equipment landing pages stay focused on published content only.
home.metaDescription: Browse published medical equipment guides, manufacturer landing pages, and equipment discovery routes.

blog.title: Published blog index
blog.description: Browse the currently published English guides, ordered by release date and ready for mobile reading first.
blog.metaDescription: Read the published English medical equipment guides available on Equip Blog.

search.title: Search published content
search.description: Search the live English content library by equipment, article title, excerpt, body text, tag, or manufacturer.
search.searchDescription: Results stay limited to published content in the active locale route and rank exact title matches ahead of weaker matches.
search.metaDescription: Search the published Equip Blog library for English medical equipment guides.

about.title: About Equip Blog
about.description: Equip Blog publishes medical equipment guides built from verified references, editorial review, and clearly stated safety limits.
about.metaDescription: Learn how Equip Blog publishes medical equipment guidance with visible references and safety boundaries.

sections.title: What the public site is for
paragraphs: Equip Blog publishes English-first guides that help readers understand what a device is, how it works, how it is maintained, and which references should be checked next.
Each public page is designed to stay readable on mobile first, then scale outward for tablet and desktop review.

sections.title: Editorial approach
paragraphs: Each public page is built from checked references, editorial review, and clearly stated limits on what can and cannot be relied on operationally.
References, disclaimers, and uncertainty stay visible so readers can move from overview reading into official manuals and source documents.

sections.title: Release 1 boundaries
items: Only locale-prefixed English public routes are active in Release 1.
Only published posts appear on public pages.
Comments and public submission controls remain visibly staged while moderation flows continue to land.
paragraphs: The route structure is already locale-ready so future translations can reuse the same layout and content patterns.

contact.title: Contact the editorial team
contact.description: Use the contact route to understand how editorial questions, corrections, partnership notes, and moderation issues will be handled for the public site.
contact.metaDescription: Review the Equip Blog contact path and editorial response expectations.

sections.title: What to contact us about
items: Factual corrections or broken reference links
Questions about scope, wording, or sourcing on a published guide
Requests related to moderation or public trust concerns
paragraphs: The public form and workflow are staged separately, but this page already holds the route and response guidance used for Release 1.

sections.title: Editorial expectations
paragraphs: When a correction request points to an official manual, manufacturer page, or similarly approved source, the editorial team should review that source before updating public content.
Readers should continue to use official documentation and institutional procedures as the operational source of truth.

sections.title: Current contact path
paragraphs: A validated public submission flow will connect to this route in a later execution step.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.description: Equip Blog is an educational publishing surface. Public pages summarize verified information but do not replace official manuals, manufacturer instructions, or institutional biomedical engineering procedures.
disclaimer.metaDescription: Read the Release 1 educational and safety disclaimer for Equip Blog public pages.

sections.title: Educational use only
paragraphs: Public content is provided for educational and informational use. It is not a substitute for official operator manuals, service manuals, manufacturer bulletins, or clinical governance requirements.
Readers should not interpret summaries, maintenance notes, or troubleshooting language on this site as authorization to bypass approved procedures.

sections.title: Safety boundary
items: Use official manufacturer documentation for exact model-specific operation.
Follow institutional biomedical engineering and clinical safety protocols.
Escalate service or safety-critical questions through qualified channels.
paragraphs: The public website intentionally keeps disclaimer language visible on post pages as well as this legal route.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.description: The privacy route explains how Release 1 public pages should treat analytics, comments, moderation records, and contact submissions once those capabilities are active.
privacy.metaDescription: Review the Equip Blog Release 1 privacy expectations for public page interactions.

sections.title: Public page data handling
paragraphs: Release 1 keeps the public site focused on reading published content, but analytics, comments, and contact flows are designed with explicit data boundaries from the start.
Any future comment or contact submission should capture only the minimum data required for moderation, response, and abuse prevention.

sections.title: Comment and moderation expectations
items: Comment bodies may be reviewed before publication.
Guest contact details should only be retained for moderation or response purposes.
Publicly visible comments should only appear after approval.
paragraphs: This policy route exists now so the legal content can evolve without changing the locale-aware public structure.

admin.title: Equip Blog Admin
admin.footer: Admin scaffold ready for authenticated workflows.

activationGuide: Add a new locale message file under src/messages.
Add the locale code to SUPPORTED_LOCALES when it is ready to go live.
Reuse the existing localization save flow to edit and persist the new locale.
localizationManagement.description: Edit the active English translation today while keeping future locales configuration-driven and schema-stable.
localizationManagement.editorDescription: Markdown, HTML, FAQ JSON, and structured content all persist per post and locale through the same storage layer.
localizationManagement.emptyPosts: No posts are available yet. Generated drafts and published posts will appear here as soon as content exists.

promptConfiguration.activeSummary: Prompt templates currently active for generation.
promptConfiguration.description: Manage the layered prompt templates that shape structured article generation, formatting, and safety boundaries.

sourceConfiguration.allowedDomainsHint: Enter one domain or wildcard domain per line. Leave blank to allow the tier to evaluate any domain.
sourceConfiguration.configurationsDescription: Priority is fixed by policy. This screen controls which source tiers are enabled and which domains are allowed for each tier.
sourceConfiguration.description: Manage approved research source tiers, allowed domains, and the exact order used by the research collector.
sourceConfiguration.enabledSummary: Source tiers currently enabled for research ingestion.
sourceConfiguration.notesHint: Describe why this tier is allowed, restricted, or temporarily disabled.

providerConfiguration.addModalDescription: Stage a new provider config in a focused form, then save the full provider configuration when you are ready.
providerConfiguration.addModalHint: This modal only adds the config to pending changes. Use Save provider configuration to persist it.
providerConfiguration.addModalSuccess: New provider config added to pending changes. Save provider configuration to persist it.
providerConfiguration.addModalValidationError: Choose a provider and model before adding the config.
providerConfiguration.apiKeyHint: API keys are encrypted on the server and never sent back to the browser. Leave this blank to keep the current key.
providerConfiguration.apiKeyPlaceholder: Paste a new API key to store securely
providerConfiguration.catalogDescription: Every provider below can be configured from the admin UI. Model suggestions come from trusted official APIs, docs, or provider-owned catalogs instead of a hard-coded model list.
providerConfiguration.credentialEnvironmentDescription: Environment-variable fallback is disabled for admin-managed provider configs.
providerConfiguration.credentialMissingDescription: This config cannot generate drafts until a stored API key is saved from this admin page.
providerConfiguration.credentialStoredDescription: A database-managed key is attached to this config and is encrypted before it is persisted.
providerConfiguration.description: Configure providers from a searchable admin workspace, load model suggestions from trusted official sources, and keep encrypted API keys on the server instead of hard-coding them in env files.
providerConfiguration.modelPlaceholder: Search or paste a model id
providerConfiguration.modelSearchHint: Model suggestions are pulled from the selected provider's trusted catalog. You can still paste an exact model id if it is not shown yet.
providerConfiguration.providerPlaceholder: Search provider key, for example openai or anthropic
providerConfiguration.providerUnknownHint: Choose one of the supported provider keys from the searchable list before saving.
providerConfiguration.purposeHint: Keep exactly one enabled fallback config available so the generator can retry automatically if the primary provider fails.
providerConfiguration.securityDescription: Generation uses only encrypted API keys saved to admin-managed provider configs. Environment-variable fallback is disabled for generation safety and traceability.
providerConfiguration.summaryDescription: The provider catalog is source-driven, searchable, and refreshable from the admin side. One enabled primary config must stay marked as default, and one enabled fallback config must remain available for automatic retry.
providerConfiguration.title: AI provider configuration

generatePost.coverageDescription: These toggles mirror the validated request contract and keep the generated article aligned with the requested scope.

generatePost.fieldErrorPrefix: Fix the highlighted fields before continuing.

generatePost.publishUnavailable: Your current role cannot publish immediately in Release 1.

generatePost.scheduleConfirmationDescription: Schedule intent is captured here, but final confirmation stays separate so the generated draft can be reviewed first.

generatePost.scheduleHint: Choosing a time here captures schedule intent in the request. Final scheduling still requires explicit confirmation after draft review.
generatePost.stageActiveHint: The backend moves through these stages in order while the current request is running.
generatePost.stageSuccessHint: The last run completed and the saved draft preview is ready for review.
generatePost.summaryDescription: This sidebar mirrors the selected request, provider, and schedule intent so the generation path stays visible while you work.

generatePost.targetAudienceHint: Choose one or more audience groups. The schema requires at least one selection.

post.defaultDisclaimer: While care has been taken to ensure accuracy of the content in this post, this content is provided for educational and informational purposes only. It does not replace the manufacturer's official instructions, operator manual, service manual, safety procedures, or institutional biomedical engineering protocols. Always follow the official manufacturer guidelines and applicable clinical regulations.
